name: HR Gatekeepers
narrativeHook: The HR department occupies a bright, open-plan office with motivational posters and potted plants. Everything feels deliberately welcoming — a mask for the surveillance apparatus underneath. Rachel Okonkwo greets you with a smile that doesn't reach her eyes.
storyArc: The agent enters the Corporate security zone and encounters HR's true function: gatekeeping through the SV-100 special vetting protocol. 37 employees across the tower are listed as 'protected' — they cannot be transferred, disciplined, or investigated without CSO approval. The agent's cover identity (Alex Morgan) has been flagged in the system as a new hire requiring 'enhanced monitoring'. Previous infiltrators have been detected at this stage. The SV-100 list maps to key conspiracy operatives across all four threads.

characters.role: Player — navigating HR vetting while maintaining cover
HR director. Administers SV-100. Loyal to Meridian. Suspicious of new hires.
Dragunov — his approval required for any action on the 37 protected employees.
Dr Tanaka referenced in counter-intelligence memos — she reviews flagged personnel files.

objectives.description: Discover the SV-100 special vetting protocol — an enhanced background check applied to employees on sensitive floors.
Identify the 37 'protected' employees who cannot be investigated without CSO approval — key operatives across all conspiracy threads.
Discover that your cover identity (Alex Morgan) has been flagged for 'enhanced monitoring' — the agent's cover may be compromised.

elevatorKey.description: The SV-100 protocol document contains an access code for the HR Archives on floor 7.

documentSpecs.description: SV-100 Special Vetting Protocol document — defines enhanced background checks, biometric verification, and psychological profiling for employees assigned to floors 36+.
List of 37 protected employees across the tower. Names, floor assignments, and the note: 'No disciplinary action, transfer, or investigation without written CSO authorisation'.
HR files including Alex Morgan's — flagged with 'ENHANCED MONITORING — NEW HIRE — MAILROOM'. Two files for previous hires who were later 'terminated' under unusual circumstances.
Rachel's correspondence with Dragunov and Dr Tanaka about personnel screening. One email: 'The new mailroom clerk matches profile patterns we've seen before. Recommend Level 2 surveillance.'
Counter-intelligence memos from Dr Tanaka's office — detection criteria for infiltrators: cover identity inconsistencies, unusual curiosity about upper floors, after-hours access patterns.
Standard HR onboarding paperwork, employee handbooks, benefits packages. Buried in these: references to 'restricted areas policy' and 'confidentiality agreements for floors 36+'.
Routine HR files — performance reviews, training records, holiday requests, payroll adjustments for ordinary employees.
HR policies — dress code, remote working, grievance procedures, diversity initiatives, mental health resources.
